chapter_title.ar: بَابُ أَنَّ الْإِمَامَ ( عليه السلام ) يَعْرِفُ الْإِمَامَ الَّذِي يَكُونُ مِنْ بَعْدِهِ وَ أَنَّ قَوْلَ اللَّهِ تَعَالَى إِنَّ اللَّهَ يَأْمُرُكُمْ أَنْ تُؤَدُّوا الْأَماناتِ إِلى أَهْلِها فِيهِمْ ( عليهم السلام ) نَزَلَتْ
chapter_title.en: Chapter 59 – The Imams<sup>asws</sup> recognises the Imams<sup>as</sup> who would happen to be from after him<sup>asws</sup>, and that the Words of Allah<sup>azwj</sup> the Exalted [4:58] Surely Allah Commands you to make over trusts to their owners, it was Revealed regarding them<sup>asws</sup>

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: صحيح - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (182/ 3)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: صحيح - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (182/ 3)
text: لَا يَمُوتُ الْإِمَامُ حَتَّى يَعْلَمَ مَنْ يَكُونُ مِنْ بَعْدِهِ فَيُوصِيَ إِلَيْهِ .

en.hubeali: Muhammad Bin Yahya, from Muhammad Bin Al Husayn, from Ibn Mahboub, from Al A’ala Bin Razeyn, from Abdullah Bin Ab Yafour,

(It has been narrated) from Abu Abdyllah<sup>asws</sup> having said: ‘An Imam<sup>asws</sup> does not pass away until he<sup>asws</sup> knows one would happen to be (an Imam<sup>asws</sup>) after him<sup>asws</sup>, so he<sup>asws</sup> bequeaths to him<sup>asws</sup>’.
en.sarwar: 5. Muhammad ibn Yahya has narrated from Muhammad ibn al-Husayn from ibn Mahbub from al-‘Ala’ ibn Razin from ‘Abdallah ibn abu Ya‘fur from abu ‘Abdallah (a.s.) who has said the following. “The does not die until he learns who the succeeding Imams is so he would deliver his will to him.”
